D) Thylacine — Australia
54. Subspecies of tiger which become recently
extinct (Pg. 263, E)
A) Bali B) Caspian
C) Javan D) All of these
55. The last 20 years alone have witnessed the
disappearance of (Pg. 263, E)
A) 300 species B) 10 species
C) 27 species D) 1000 species
56. Match the following- (Pg. 264, E)
Species % of threat of
a
EIEN EY ES
b|Mammals [232% |
[| Gymnosperms [4 [31% |
A) a-1, b-3, c-2,d-4
B) a-3,b-1,c-2,d-4
C) a-3, b-1, c-4,d-2
D) a-3, b-4, c-1,d-2
57. Select the correct statement about ‘Sixth
Extinction’ (Pg. 264, E)
A) The extinction rates are estimated to be
100 - 1000 times faster than in the pre-
human times.
B) Human activities are responsible for
the faster rates.
C) Half of all the species on Earth might
be wiped out within the next 1000
years.
D) Both A and B option are correct.
58. Loss of biodiversity in a region may lead to
(Pg. 264, E)
A) decline in plant production,
B) lowered resistance to environmental.
perturbations such as drought.
C) Increased variability in ecosystem
process.
D) All of these.
59. The Evil Quartet’ is the term used to
describe (Pg. 264, E)
A) Causes of habitat losses
B) Causes of biodiversity losses.
C) Causes of water losses
D) All of these.
60. The accelerated rates of species
extinctions are largely due to (Pg. 264, E)
